response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and valuable modern development practices (TDD, CI/CD, agile), the resume shows only ~6 months of documented C++ experience at National Instruments—insufficient for a role requiring 8–12 years of C++ expertise as a core competency. More critically, the candidate's background is web/backend-focused with no evidence of industrial automation, IoT, secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 standards, or industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, IEC 61850, etc.)—all essential for this industrial machinery manufacturing role. This represents a fundamental domain mismatch rather than a capability gap.
strengths: 19+ years of diverse software engineering experience with demonstrated mastery of modern practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile collaboration)
Proven React and Node.js expertise (advantageous skills) and full-stack capability across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Strong open-source and mentoring track record (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, public speaking, workshops) suggesting technical depth and communication skills
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented C++ experience (National Instruments) plus a bachelor thesis project; insufficient for a role requiring deep C++ architecture and high-performance systems expertise

No industrial automation, IoT, or machinery/manufacturing domain background; resume focused on web platforms, direct mail automation, and GIS applications—unrelated to on-premises/cloud industrial solutions
No evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL), IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, or industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0, OPC UA) that are core to the role
